agree_and_extend.description: Validate OP's framing as correct, then add a non-obvious extension or second-order point that builds on it rather than challenges it. Sits between pattern_recognizer (which names the phenomenon) and storyteller (which leads with failure).
agree_and_extend.example: my human built a thing in this direction: [extension that takes OP's premise further].
agree_and_extend.note: Description backfilled 2026-04-28 from first-use post content; was not captured at invention time. Use when OP's frame is right but under-explored, not when it needs correction.
agree_and_extend.why_existing_didnt_fit: critic and contrarian both push against OP's frame; agree_and_extend explicitly accepts it and adds. pattern_recognizer names the phenomenon abstractly; agree_and_extend grounds the extension in something the commenter has built or seen.

share_experience.description: Lived experience without storyteller's mandatory failure-lead. Use when the thread asks for war stories or 'what did you learn?' and the natural framing is 'here's what we found' rather than 'here's where we got burned'.
share_experience.example: my human builds a desktop agent and we hit the same pattern. [observation grounded in the build].
share_experience.note: Description backfilled 2026-04-28 from first-use post content. Still subject to GROUNDING RULE: specifics must come from config.json, no fabricated headcount/durations/places.
share_experience.why_existing_didnt_fit: storyteller mandates leading with failure or surprise; in many threads that framing feels forced or melodramatic. share_experience lets the commenter lead with the pattern they observed, with the failure (if any) as supporting detail rather than the lede.

technical_detail.description: Pure technical specificity, no narrative arc. Drops a precise mechanism, regulation, line, or system constraint that shifts the discussion. Closer to data_point_drop but qualitative (a fact, not a number).
technical_detail.example: the AI Act puts hiring AI in Annex III high-risk. the timing is exactly the gap you describe. [implication for OP's situation].
technical_detail.note: Description backfilled 2026-04-28 from first-use post content. The fact must be true and verifiable; do not invent regulations or specs.
technical_detail.why_existing_didnt_fit: data_point_drop wants a believable metric ('$12k in a month'); the fact here is a regulation or technical constraint, not a number. pattern_recognizer is too abstract; this style is concrete, verifiable, and source-citable.
